nodes.context: Organized crime syndicate in the Philippines, originally an anti-communist vigilante group.
Group that originally established Kuratong Baleleng in 1986.
Muslim Maguindanaon guerrilla groups that had ties to Kuratong Baleleng.
Military force that handpicked the first leader of Kuratong Baleleng.
Government commission that killed 11 members of Kuratong Baleleng in 1995.
Human rights law firm that prosecuted the PACC officers in the 1995 murder case.

edges.evidence_quote: The group was originally established in 1986 by the Botanical Youth Club
edges.context: Botanical Youth Club founded Kuratong Baleleng.

edges.evidence_quote: ties to the Maguindanao guerrillas led Muslim Maguindanaon clans to join
edges.context: Kuratong Baleleng had ties to Maguindanao guerrillas, leading to Muslim clans joining.

edges.evidence_quote: Its first leader was Octavio "Ongkoy" Parojinog, handpicked by the Armed Forces of the Philippines
edges.context: AFP handpicked the first leader of Kuratong Baleleng.

edges.evidence_quote: 11 members of Kuratong Baleleng were killed by the forces of the Presidential Anti-Crime Commission
edges.context: PACC killed 11 members of Kuratong Baleleng in 1995.

edges.evidence_quote: Officers were prosecuted with the Free Legal Assistance Group (FLAG), the largest human rights law firm, serving as the chief prosecutor in the case.
edges.context: FLAG prosecuted PACC officers for the killing of Kuratong Baleleng members.
